beats.head: Castlegear Group Adds Equipment Order to Multi-Hospital Tray Pact
beats.body: Castlegear expanded its hospital supply relationship with an order for sterilization and instrument-handling equipment valued near $340M over five years. Sable Brandt said the follow-on validates the tray system rollout and deepens ties with the same hospital network group. Deliveries begin next quarter.

beats.head: Hearthstone Group Beats Again as Refillable Lines Broaden Reach
beats.body: Hearthstone reported quarterly revenue up 11% with operating margins widening for a third straight period, crediting rising reorder rates on its refillable Jansan and household ranges. Aurelia Aldous said institutional accounts now make up nearly a third of concentrate volumes. The company nudged its full-year outlook higher.

beats.head: Holtware Co. Extends Lifetime Warranty to New Power-Tool Range
beats.body: Holtware broadened its flagship guarantee to a debut line of corded and cordless power tools, marking its first move beyond hand tools. Aurelia Dray said the range targets the same tradespeople driving showroom traffic, with initial stocking across the recently expanded retail footprint. Pricing sits modestly above competing tiers.

beats.head: Korrworks Extends Merrick Line Pact to Cover Third Depot
beats.body: Korrworks widened its handling agreement with rail operator Merrick Line, adding its newest Brae Harbor depot to the arrangement for an estimated $1.4B over six years. Rhys Throne said the expansion locks in throughput across all three depots and positions the network for a fourth site under review. The deal takes effect next quarter.

beats.head: Korrwright Group Adds Wire Line to Keep Pace With Verant Backlog
beats.body: Korrwright will commission a new high-capacity wire-drawing line at its Halden works to service the growing Verant cluster mandate, a $220M investment set to double output by year-end. Halden Fenn said the expansion answers a backlog that has stretched delivery windows, with the first spools expected within three quarters. The move follows two consecutive guidance lifts.

beats.head: Lhaus Industries Debuts Fast-Charge Cell Pack With Record Cycle Life
beats.body: Lhaus unveiled a second-generation cell pack it says retains 90% capacity after 4,000 cycles while accepting an 80% charge in under twelve minutes. Chief engineer Marta Voss said the breakthrough came from a new electrode chemistry developed at the company's Fenwick lab, and Cole Brandt hinted the technology could underpin a broader push beyond fleet customers. Analysts called it the most significant Power & Storage advance since the division launched.

beats.head: Steg Tooling Tops Forecasts as Supply Pact Fills Order Book
beats.body: Steg posted a 9% revenue gain as its multi-year fastener pact with two assembly partners moved into full delivery, lifting utilization at both plants above 85%. Iris Vossen said fixed-price terms held margins steady despite input cost pressure. The company reaffirmed guidance and flagged tooling upgrades for the second plant.

beats.head: Vossenstead Supply Breaks Ground on Third Distribution Hub
beats.body: Vossenstead began construction on a third hub sited between its existing food-service and plumbing operations, a $95M project intended to shorten regional delivery routes. Bram Thorne said the site preserves the twin-hub efficiencies while adding capacity ahead of demand. Completion is targeted within eighteen months.

beats.head: Boreas Power Breaks Ground on $5.2B Compute-and-Storage Campus at Hollowmere
beats.body: Mira Thorne unveiled the house's most ambitious project to date, pairing a massive compute complex with grid-scale storage at the expanded Hollowmere site. The build folds in the momentum from Calderwick integration and Phase Three, targeting first power within 18 months and creating an estimated 2,400 permanent roles. Thorne said Boreas was 'done waiting for demand to slow down, because it won't.'

beats.head: Brunstone & Sons Pivots to Storage With Debut of Lambourne-Era Vault Cells
beats.body: Fresh off the emergency reset, new CEO Royce Lambourne steered the house away from vehicles and toward its Power & Storage arm, unveiling the Vault modular battery line. The launch leans on Brunstone's steadier storage business rather than another consumer-facing gamble, with initial capacity of 120,000 units earmarked for commercial buyers. Analysts framed it as a deliberately smaller bet after the Meridian collapse.

beats.head: Fennmoor Signs Hospital Network Rollout for Larkspur One Diagnostics System
beats.body: Dorian Brandt landed a multi-site deployment pact placing the Threll-sensor-based Larkspur One across a 22-hospital network, valued at roughly $310M over five years. The deal validates the recent product launch and pulls the acquired sensor unit into steady recurring revenue. Brandt signaled appetite for further tuck-in buys to widen the diagnostics platform.

beats.head: Korrwright Works Buys Rival Heritage Maker Sennfeld in Landmark Deal
beats.body: Dunmore Carrow announced the $2.1B acquisition of storied competitor Sennfeld Chronometers, folding its ateliers and movement archive into the Velloux operation. The purchase, Korrwright's largest ever, doubles its skilled watchmaker headcount and secures a century of proprietary complications. Carrow said the combined house would 'define the top of the market,' capping a run that began with the Velloux launch and its Timepiece of the Year win.

beats.head: Marrowmoor Holdings Crosses 100-Contract Mark With Riverside Cluster Win
beats.body: Dorian Steg's quiet buildout reached a milestone as the house signed a cluster of five servicing contracts across the Riverside precinct, pushing its active book past 100 properties. The deals add an estimated $48M in annual recurring revenue and deepen Marrowmoor's Cleaning & Jansan supply pipeline. Steg reiterated his preference for 'growth you barely notice until it's everywhere.'

beats.head: Palettstone & Sons Extends Heirloom Pro to National Chain After Strong Regional Sell-Through
beats.body: Building on brisk regional numbers, Cole Fenn secured a national shelf-placement deal putting the award-winning Heirloom Pro line in more than 800 stores. The agreement is expected to roughly triple annual unit volume for the range, with Palettstone adding a second production shift to meet demand. Fenn called the regional pilot 'the proof we needed to go wide.'

beats.head: Thronehaus & Co. Opens Aldren-Backed Casing Foundry in Verrand
beats.body: Cora Dray cut the ribbon on a 40,000-square-foot casing foundry built around the newly acquired Aldren team, consolidating shell and bracelet work under one roof. The site is projected to lift in-house component output by roughly 35% within its first year and marks the first physical footprint added since the Sovereign Collection launch. Dray called it 'the next hungry step,' hinting at further supplier acquisitions to follow.

beats.head: Bruncrest Works Ousts CEO Corwin Hale as Losses Mount and Board Loses Patience
beats.body: Bruncrest Works' board removed CEO Corwin Hale after a fourth consecutive disappointing quarter, ending a turbulent tenure marked by the collapsed Meridian pact and a costly overhaul. The company named turnaround veteran Ines Marlow as chief executive, tasking her with stabilizing finances and settling the outstanding Meridian litigation. Shares had shed nearly 60% of their value since the Meridian dispute erupted.

beats.head: Kessel Drive Sued as Fleet Client Alleges Breach Over Recalled Storage Packs
beats.body: The national fleet operator behind Kessel Drive's $3.2 billion touring pact filed suit, alleging breach of contract and seeking damages after the overheating-driven recall grounded thousands of units. CEO Dorian Brandt vowed to fight the claim while accelerating a redesigned storage pack he said would be 'faster and safer than anything on the road.' The dispute threatens the largest deal in the company's history.

beats.head: Marrowstone Build Hit With $310 Million Judgment in Embankment Collapse Case
beats.body: A tribunal ordered Marrowstone Build to pay $310 million in damages over the embankment collapse, finding the company liable for inadequate site inspections despite its recent safety-division overhaul. CEO Bram Halloran said the firm would appeal portions of the ruling while honoring victim compensation immediately. The judgment marks the costliest single liability in the company's century-long history.

beats.head: Vossenspire Slapped With Record Fine as Blackout Probe Finds Deferred Maintenance
beats.body: Regulators concluded their investigation into the two-region blackout, levying a $480 million penalty and citing years of deferred grid maintenance and understaffed control rooms. The findings directly implicate cost-cutting under the prior leadership, intensifying pressure on new CEO Halden Roe, who called the report 'a hard but necessary reckoning.' Vossenspire agreed to a binding three-year remediation plan under external oversight.

beats.head: Halhold Works Unveils Sweeping Overhaul as New Chief Vale Takes Control
beats.body: Gunnar Vale announced a full restructuring that splits the troubled Verity Pro medical-equipment unit into a ring-fenced division and cuts 900 roles company-wide. Vale set aside 260 million to settle the hospital litigation and pledged an independent safety audit of all device lines. The plan drew a mixed reception amid warnings the transition period will be 'painful and turbulent.'

beats.head: Marlgear Supply Hit With Class Action as Drennan Mills Injuries Mount
beats.body: A consortium of plaintiffs filed a class action seeking 480 million in damages, citing the leaked memos showing Atlas defects were flagged before launch. Halden Vossen, whose public defense of the line drew scrutiny, now faces calls from three major shareholders for an independent board review. Regulators signaled the safety probe could escalate to formal charges.

beats.head: Greywater Grid Lands Sweeping Multi-Region Power-and-Compute Contract Worth 1.2 Billion Marks
beats.body: Greywater Grid unveiled its largest deal ever, a 1.2-billion-mark agreement to build and supply three compute campuses paired with dedicated storage across four regions. CEO Vale Halloran called it a defining moment that roughly doubles the company's committed backlog. The buildout will lean on Greywater's record-density storage blocks and span the next five years.

beats.head: Gromford Works Ousts Mira Ashby as Board Moves to Contain Aurelian Noir Fallout
beats.body: After 128 years of famously restless leadership, Mira Ashby stepped down under board pressure as the concealment inquiry deepened and the Aurelian Noir recall topped 60,000 units. The board named longtime operations chief Delphine Vaugh to steer the house through the widening regulatory probe, promising an internal review of every timepiece line. Vaugh takes over with a settlement fund reportedly reaching 40 million marks.

beats.head: Blackhold Craters as Greycliff Suit and Halted Test Bank Gut Quarterly Results
beats.body: Blackhold reported a quarterly loss of $214 million, swinging from a slim profit a year earlier, as the suspended Greycliff test bank stripped projected storage revenue and legal reserves ballooned to $90 million. CEO Tomas Vren conceded the Greycliff timeline had 'slipped indefinitely' and withdrew full-year guidance. The investor suit over safety filings now moves to discovery, with three institutional backers reportedly weighing whether to join.

beats.head: Castlestone's Karsh Halts Second Campus, Cuts 900 Jobs in Sweeping Turnaround
beats.body: New CEO Pell Karsh froze construction on the second Vellmont campus and eliminated 900 roles, roughly 12% of staff, as part of a $1.1 billion writedown aimed at stemming the twin-campus losses. Karsh said the company would consolidate compute output at the completed site and refocus on the Vellmont order book rather than expansion. He pledged a return to positive cash flow within four quarters.

beats.head: Varnvale & Sons Buys Bellmont Master Casemaker in 880-Million Vertical Play
beats.body: Restless as ever, Bram Wold has folded the storied Halvren Case Atelier into Varnvale for 880 million, giving the house control of both dials and casings for its flagship Bellmont line. The deal secures the last independent supplier of Varnvale's signature guilloché cases and marks Wold's third acquisition in eighteen months. Analysts note the maker now owns nearly every step of its timepiece production.

beats.head: Blackton Industries Sued by Halvern as Weld Defect Fallout Escalates
beats.body: Halvern filed a $650M suit against Blackton, alleging the defective weld batch forced a costly production halt and breached supply warranties. CEO Mara Mercer disputed the figure but confirmed Blackton has set aside $180M in reserves as the safety probe continues. The dispute threatens a contract that accounts for nearly a third of Blackton's annual revenue.

beats.head: Castlehold Rocked as Saltmere Indictments Name Former Executives Over Falsified Fault Logs
beats.body: Prosecutors unsealed charges against three former Castlehold engineers and ex-operations chief Darel Skarn, alleging systematic falsification of Saltmere fault-reporting records dating back to 2023. CEO Ivo Reneth pledged full cooperation and announced a $420M charge to cover legal reserves and remediation, calling the disclosures 'a wound we will not hide from.' Shares slid as the company confirmed Saltmere will remain offline through at least next year.
